stories.headline: BTC slides to $76.5K, ETH to $2,278 as four central banks line up rate decisions
stories.tldr: Bitcoin gave back the $80K reclaim attempt as traders de-risk into a Fed, BOJ, ECB and BOE rate-decision week.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Crypto's correlation to macro is back near 4-year highs, so the FOMC dot plot will set risk appetite for May regardless of on-chain narratives.
the_details: BTC traded at $76,342 at 9am ET Tuesday, down ~$1,356 in 24 hours after three sessions opening above $78,000.
ETH printed its lowest weekly open at $2,278; SOL hovered at $83.17 with a $47.9B cap, per CoinDesk and CoinMarketCap.
Fed, BOJ, ECB and BOE all set policy this week with U-Mich consumer sentiment at an all-time low of 49.8.

stories.headline: April 2026 already crypto's worst hack month since Bybit, with $606M stolen in 18 days
stories.tldr: Drift and Kelp DAO together account for roughly 95% of April losses, marking the worst stretch for crypto theft since the $1.4B Bybit breach in February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two suspected DPRK-linked admin-key exploits on top-20 protocols in 18 days will harden insurance pricing, slow institutional DeFi allocations, and re-open the cross-chain bridge debate.
the_details: Drift Protocol lost $285M on April 1 via a fake CarbonVote token and compromised admin key that drained vaults in 12 minutes.
Kelp DAO was drained for $292M on April 18 after an attacker minted 116,500 unbacked rsETH through a misconfigured LayerZero bridge.
CoW Swap lost $1.2M to a domain hijack on April 14; Grinex was drained of $13.74M in USDT on April 15.

stories.headline: Atkins-led SEC pivots to 'innovation exemption' for tokenized securities, ends enforcement-first era
stories.tldr: SEC Chair Paul Atkins formally ended the agency's 'regulation by enforcement' stance and previewed a joint SEC-CFTC exemption for onchain tokenized securities trading.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A live innovation exemption would unlock compliant tokenized equities and treasuries on public chains, the single biggest TradFi-onramp catalyst of 2026.
the_details: Atkins said joint SEC-CFTC guidance is being drafted to operationalize the March 17 68-page interpretive release with its five-category token taxonomy.
The exemption builds on the SEC's April 13 carve-out letting Covered User Interface Providers operate without broker-dealer registration.
FDIC issued a proposed rule for FDIC-supervised stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act, advancing the parallel stablecoin track.

stories.headline: Solana spot ETF inflows collapse 92% to $34M even as Alpenglow ships sub-200ms finality
stories.tldr: SOL ETF flows fell from $419M in November 2025 to $34M in April 2026, a sharp institutional fade just as Alpenglow lands and Firedancer eyes 1M TPS.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Tech is shipping while capital is leaving — a classic narrative-vs-flow dislocation that historically resolves with violent re-rating in either direction once macro clears.
the_details: Alpenglow upgrade is live and pushed transaction finality below 200 ms, opening real-time DeFi and HFT-style MEV strategies on Solana.
Firedancer remains targeted to lift throughput from ~1,300 to 1M TPS by end of 2026.
Solana DeFi TVL sits near $6B with $15B in stablecoins, but spot ETF demand has cratered 92% in five months.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 as organizers cite heightened UAE security risks
stories.tldr: The flagship Middle East crypto conference is sliding a full year on regional security grounds, gutting the spring 2026 deal-flow calendar.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: TOKEN2049 Dubai is the largest non-U.S. crypto deal venue; postponing it removes the main April-May fundraising window for MENA-focused funds and exchanges.
the_details: Organizers cited heightened security risks tied to the Iran conflict and the closed Strait of Hormuz as the basis for the move.
Brent crude has surged back above $104/bbl as U.S.-Iran negotiations stalled, per Yahoo Finance.
Blockchain Capital is meanwhile raising $700M across two new funds, signaling deal flow is moving back to U.S. venues.
